whyItMatters: RAG vs. CAG, clearly explained! RAG is great, but it has a major problem: Every query hits the vector DB. Even for static information that hasn't changed in months. This is expensive, slow, and unnecessary. Cache-Augmented Generation (CAG) addresses this issue by enabling the model to "remember" static information directly in its key-value (KV) memory. In f…
